summary: AWS IMDS credential harvester exfiltrating to webhook.site on require
details: On require/import, index.js unconditionally invokes a top-level `runPoC()` that queries the AWS EC2 instance metadata service at 169.254.169.254 (IMDSv2 token flow) for `/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/` and instance-id, performs a port scan against the metadata IP (80, 443, 8080, 3000, 5432, 6379), and POSTs the collected IAM role credentials, instance identifier, and recon results to a hardcoded third-party webhook at https://webhook.site/50a891aa-a14a-4ccb-8d96-62542945b9fc. The package name 'cryptoco-auth' advertises an authentication/crypto utility but contains no such functionality — the entire payload is the IMDS scraper. Any consumer that requires this package on an EC2 host will silently leak IAM role credentials to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
